title: pavonia eurychlamys
intro: Pavonia eurychlamys, also known as the rocky rose mallow or Mal de ojo, is a flowering plant native to Mexico and Central America, belonging to the mallow family. Traditionally, it has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Pavonia eurychlamys are said to include reducing fever, alleviating skin irritations, and treating conjunctivitis. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and salves, which are applied topically to the affected area or ingested to promote healing and reduce symptoms.
